channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: All 007 First Light Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained
item.content:encoded:
You might think it’s a simple task to place an order for upcoming games, but with the amount of editions and bonuses up for grabs these days, it’s becoming increasingly tough. The same could be said for 007 First Light, the upcoming Bond title that has numerous editions on offer.

Now, you not only need to decide whether you want to buy the game or not, but you also need to figure out which edition to go for! Admittedly, 007 First Light has some pretty awesome editions, so if you’re curious what each one includes and how much they cost, you’re in the right place.

How Much Does 007 First Light Cost?

The standard edition of 007 First Light will cost the usual $69.99, as it runs the same price as the usual AAA title.

Of course, you’ll have to contend with regional pricing, so that price may vary depending on where you live. This edition entitles you to all base-game content available at launch, including the full campaign.

  • More Insider Gaming: 007 First Light Release Date & All Platforms, Explained

However, if you’re after a little bit more, and you get in there in time, you can pre-order the game and get some extra goodies.

Does 007 First Light Have Pre-Order Bonuses?

Yes, 007 First Light does have pre-order bonuses, and all you need to do is pre-order the standard edition of the game.

If you decide to pre-order the game, not only will you be given a handful of cosmetics, but you’ll also be granted 24-hour early access to the game! Typically, you’d need to pre-order a bumper edition of a game to get such rewards, but pre-ordering the standard edition gives you all the benefits of the Deluxe Edition at no extra cost, which includes:

  • 24-hour early access
  • Four Outfits:
    • Day of the Dead
    • Desert Explorer
    • Silent Anchor
    • Gentleman Operator
  • Weapon Skin:
    • Agent’s Mark
  • Gleaming Pack, including four gadget skins:
    • Gleaming Lighter
    • Gleaming Earphones
    • Gleaming Dart Gun
    • Gleaming Pen

All of that, and we haven’t even unpacked the other editions of the game yet!

Every 007 First Light Edition—All Contents, Explained

There are four editions of 007 First Light available for purchase, with the Standard being the cheapest, and the Legacy Edition being the most expensive.

You’d imagine that the Collector’s Edition would be the most expensive version of the game on offer, but the Legacy Edition takes things to another level. This rendition of the game has a golden theme, with an incredible Golden Gun Figurine stealing the show. It’ll set you back a whopping $300, and it’s definitely one reserved for the 007 superfans out there.

  • More Insider Gaming: How Long Is 007 First Light? Game Length & Total Hours, Explained

For details on each edition of 007 First Light, check out the table below:

Which edition of 007 First Light will you be purchasing? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Sony PlayStation Lawsuit Refund: How To Check Eligibility and Claim Your Money
  • Invincible VS Complete Character Tier List
  • All Free Characters in Neverness to Everness & How to Get Them

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Datamine Uncovers New Map, Trading, Events, and More
item.content:encoded:
ARC Raiders players everywhere are contending with the mixed reception of Riven Tides, the update that brought an end to the Escalation roadmap, a four-month pipeline that went out with more of a fizzle than a bang. Many of them are now focused on what’s coming next, hopeful that whatever is lined up will be more substantial.

In a recent post from a dataminer, it was suggested that there is plenty on the horizon. This post went mostly under the radar but is now starting to circulate, uncovering references to the next ARC Raiders map, the next ARC construct, upcoming events, a trading hub, a clan system, and much more.

Pinch of Salt Time, ARC Fans

Datamining efforts should always be taken with a considerable pinch of salt. They’re based on information that anyone, with the right know-how, can obtain, and they’re almost always concerning things that are subject to change, and often do. At no point is this article to be taken as gospel!

In a recent post on pastebin, one dataminer spooled out a stack of upcoming ARC Raiders features, starting with the next new map. It took five months for Embark Studios to bring in the first new map since Stella Montis, so the hope is that this map, reportedly named Frozen Trail, will arrive much sooner.

The post details a string of points of interest in the Frozen Trail map, including:

  • Central Station
  • Factory
  • Loading Platform
  • Observatory
  • Railyard
  • Village

Given the scale of those POIs, that could be one hell of a large map. That would be appreciated, given Riven Tides’ underwhelming makeup.

The post also detailed new map conditions, one of which appeared in the rumor mill several months ago:

  • Toxic Swamp: Geothermal fissures have erupted beneath the swamp, releasing both toxic gases and rare natural resources.
  • Acid Rain: An especially sour rain corrodes everything on the surface, harming any Raider caught outdoors.
  • Heat Wave: A scorching sun has been beating down on everything and everyone, making it exhausting just to get around. Stay in the shadows if you can.
  • The Queens: Multiple Queens have been spotted in the area, though no Harvester seems to be anywhere nearby.

There was another minor map condition that suggested extraction points would be buttoned up tight and needing repair before they could be successfully used.

Elsewhere, a reference to a new trading mechanic appeared in the data:

At the shady SPERANZA PIAZZA you will be able to trade with other RAIDERS.

This was noted as being under construction, but it is not the first time that we’ve seen a peer-to-peer trading solution surface in the ARC Raiders rumors space.

More gadgets, all of which were based on instruments, were listed, as were two new weapons: Stanza and Malleus.

A new ARC Operation called Dead Reckoning was named. This would see a new ‘Frigate’ ARC surface, one that’s reportedly large enough to fly around the map and be boarded by Raiders. In the data, it was suggested that this would be based on the cryptic UFOs seen flying around during the Shrouded Sky update.

Finally, there was a mention of a clan system, which would make sense, given the community-focused aspects of ARC Raiders. The dataminer pasted a reference to a clan ‘playstyles’ string that would allow users to pick the focus of their clan. This can be questing, PvP, PvE, or any.

Again, I’ll stress that this information is based on a dataminer post and shouldn’t be taken as a guarantee that any of this will surface in ARC Raiders. But what do you think? What item on this breakdown would you most like to see in ARC Raiders? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders now has regional pricing

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Horizon Hunters Gathering Second Playtest Details Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Horizon Hunters Gathering will be hosted this month, with new heroes, a playable episode, and more.

Guerilla Games held the first closed playtest for the game on PS5 and PC in February 2026, with players getting to explore three playable characters and two game modes. The upcoming playtest will add two more characters to the mix, introduce the game’s campaign, a new region, and additional difficulties.

Horizon Hunters Gathering Second Playtest Improves On Previous Playtest While Adding New Content

The second playtest for Horizon Hunters Gathering will be held from May 22 to 25 and will be available only on PS5 and PC. The only way to get into the playtest is by registering through the PlayStation Beta Program. Coming to the content included in the upcoming playtests, players will see the arrival of two new characters: Ensa, a charismatic Oseram smuggler with a mercenary past, and Shadow, a Carja covert operative who commands a fearsome Stalker machine.

The next focus of the playtest is Episodes, which provide an entry into the game’s narrative while revealing new mysteries, characters, and mechanics. As a Hunter, players will have to work towards saving Ashwater Valley from a horde of machines. Also, the Machine Incursion and Cauldron Descent modes return with added difficulties.

Coming to the new region, the Horizon Hunters Gathering second playtest will feature Breakers’ Bounty, where players can explore “dense jungles and ravaged ruins bordering a scorching desert home to dangerous machines.” Players will retain access to the Gathering and Devil’s Thirst in the upcoming playtest.

Lastly, Training Modules have been added to improve general onboarding for the game and its systems, which include Hunter NPCs that provide battle assists and fill-up spots in 3-player parties.

What are your thoughts on Horizon Hunters Gathering’s second playtest? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox Game Pass May 2026 Wave 1 games. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
